mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% when rates rise.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack signals impending earnings deterioration, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal spending and consumer prices indicate robust demand outpacing supply, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL yield negative forward returns in greater than 50% of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase signals expanding capacity utilization and margin improvement, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.1) during recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained economic expansion and rising household income, supporting consumer spending and positive equity forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp year-over-year contraction in housing starts signals a collapsing construction sector and reduced household wealth, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% year-over-year
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of quarters following a greater than 10% YoY drop in HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while inflation accelerates, real borrowing costs increase and corporate margins compress, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Combined rate hikes and inflation spikes yield positive forward returns in greater than 60% of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3% during a mid-cycle expansion confirms strong aggregate demand, leading to earnings upgrades and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth greater than 3% fails to generate positive 60-day forward returns with a win rate above 55% during mid-cycle periods.
